---
pagetitle: "Interactive Tools for Exploring SLC's Flight Network"
---

# Interactive Tools for Exploring SLC's Flight Network

This page provides access to three companion Shiny apps for the *CHANCE* article *Tracking and Mapping Change in Salt Lake City's Flight Network, 1987--2026*. The apps support interactive exploration of airport connections, flight-volume patterns, and spatial structure in the SLC flight network.

:::::: grid
::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
### SLC Flight Network Explorer

Explore the airports connected to Salt Lake City over time. The app maps SLC's flight network by year or across all years, supports airline-level filtering, displays optional route lines, and provides various airport-level details.

[Launch app](https://9ixde5-aleksey-kulyukin.shinyapps.io/slc-flight-network/){.btn .btn-primary}
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
### SLC Flight Volume Explorer

Examine daily flight-volume patterns for SLC. The app separates main flights from diverted flights, shows date-level observations relative to each year's average daily count, and identifies unusually large diverted flight observations using the app's outlier threshold.

[Launch app](https://9ixde5-aleksey-kulyukin.shinyapps.io/slc-flight-volume/){.btn .btn-primary}
:::

::: {.g-col-12 .g-col-md-4}
### Airport and Weather Station Spatial Explorer

Compare the spatial distribution of airports and weather stations over time. The app maps airport and station locations by year and provides point-pattern visualizations and tests, including Ripley-Rasson area plots, quadrat counts, kernel density maps, nearest-neighbor envelope plots, quadrat tests, and Clark-Evans tests.

[Launch app](https://cam-swapp.shinyapps.io/spatial_app/){.btn .btn-primary}
:::
::::::
